subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install the gems specified by the Gemfile or Gemfile.lock

options.description: Create binstubs in dir
Remove unused gems after install
For Production and CI use
Redownload all gems
Do not allow lock file to update
Cache the full index locally
The gemfile to use
Maximum number of parallel installs
Use only gems already downloaded or cached
Do not use vendor/cache
Do not remove stale gems
Path the install gems too
Do not print to stdout
Retry failed network requests N times
Uses the specified ruby executable for binstubs
Makes a bundle that can work without depending on Rubygems or Bundler at runtime
Use system Rubygems location
Apply the Rubygems security policy
Groups to install
Groups to NOT install

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update dependencies to their latest versions

options.description: Update all gems specified in Gemfile
Only update the gems in the specified group
The name of a :git or :path source used in the Gemfile
Use only gems already downloaded or cached
Update the locked version of Ruby to the current version of Ruby
Update the locked version of bundler to the invoked bundler version
Fall back to using the single-file index of all gems
Specify the number of jobs to run in parallel. The default is 1
Retry failed network or git requests for number times
Only output warnings and errors
Force downloading every gem
Prefer updating only to next patch version
Prefer updating only to next minor version
Prefer updating to next major version (default)
Do not allow any gem to be updated past latest --patch | --minor | --major
Do not allow shared dependencies to be updated

subcommands.name: package
subcommands.description: Package the .gem files required by your application into the vendor/cache directory

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command in the context of the bundle

options.description: Pass all file descriptors to the new process

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add gem to the Gemfile and run bundle install

options.description: Specify version requirements
Specify the group(s) for the added gem
Specify the source
Adds the gem to the Gemfile but does not install it
Adds optimistic declaration of version
Adds strict declaration of version

subcommands.name: binstubs
subcommands.description: Install the binstubs of the listed gems

options.description: Overwrite existing binstubs
The location to install the specified binstubs to
Makes binstubs that can work without depending on Rubygems or Bundler at runtime
Specify a different shebang executable name than the default

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Determine whether the requirements for your application are installed and available to Bundler

options.description: Locks the Gemfile before running the command
Use the specified gemfile instead of the Gemfile
Specify a different path than the system default

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show the source location of a particular gem in the bundle

options.description: List the paths of all gems that are required by your Gemfile

subcommands.name: outdated
subcommands.description: Show all of the outdated gems in the current bundle

options.description: Do not attempt to fetch gems remotely and use the gem cache instead
Check for newer pre-release gems
Check against a specific source
Only list newer versions allowed by your Gemfile requirements
Use minimal formatting for more parseable output
List gems from a specific group
List gems organized by groups
Strict conservative resolution, do not allow any gem to be updated past latest --patch | --minor| --major
Prefer updating only to next minor version
Prefer updating to next major version (default)
Prefer updating only to next patch version
Only list major newer versions
Only list minor newer versions
Only list patch newer versions
Only list gems specified in your Gemfile, not their dependencies

subcommands.name: console
subcommands.description: Start an IRB session in the current bundle

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: Open an installed gem in the editor

subcommands.description: Generate a lockfile for your dependencies

options.description: Ignores the existing lockfile
Do not attempt to connect to rubygems.org

Prints the lockfile to STDOUT instead of writing to the file
 system
The path where the lockfile should be written to
Fall back to using the single-file index of all gems

Add a new platform to the lockfile, re-resolving for the addi-
 tion of that platform
Remove a platform from the lockfile
If updating, prefer updating only to next patch version
If updating, prefer updating only to next minor version
If updating, prefer updating to next major version (default)
If updating, do not allow any gem to be updated past latest --patch | --minor | --major

subcommands.name: viz
subcommands.description: Generate a visual representation of your dependencies

options.description: The name to use for the generated file. See --format option
This is output format option
Set to show the version of each required dependency
Set to show each gem version
Exclude gems that are part of the specified named group

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Generate a simple Gemfile, placed in the current directory

options.description: Use the specified .gemspec to create the Gemfile

subcommands.name: gem
subcommands.description: Create a simple gem, suitable for development with Bundler

options.description: Specify that Bundler should create a binary executable
Do not create a binary

Do not create a CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md

Do not add C extension code
Add an MIT license
Do not create a LICENSE.txt
Specify the test framework that Bundler should use
Open the resulting gemspec in EDITOR

options.description: Print the changes, but do not clean the unused gems
Force a clean even if --path is not set
Only output warnings and errors
The location of the Gemfile which Bundler should use
